description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the docker:latest image with the docker:latest-dind service that follows the Auto DevOps template. Define stages build, test, code_quality, sast, dependency_scanning, container_scanning, license_management, dast, review, staging, production, and cleanup. In the build job log in to the GitLab Container Registry (docker login -u $CI_REGISTRY_USER -p $CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD $CI_REGISTRY), then either docker build -t $CI_APPLICATION_REPOSITORY:$CI_APPLICATION_TAG . if a Dockerfile exists or use gliderlabs/herokuish to build a Heroku‑style image, and push the image to the registry. The test job runs the project’s test script (e.g., ./run-tests.sh) and stores test reports as artifacts. The code_quality, sast, dependency_scanning, container_scanning and license_management jobs each run their respective security‑scanner Docker images from registry.gitlab.com/gitlab-org/security-products with the source code mounted, producing JSON or JUnit reports as artifacts. The review, staging and production jobs deploy the built image to a Kubernetes cluster using helm upgrade --install with values derived from CI variables (image.repository, image.tag, replicaCount, postgresql settings, etc.), and are triggered only on merge‑request pipelines or the main branch respectively; all jobs use appropriate caches and keep artifacts for later stages.
